summary: This is an extremely brief, whimsical post about Jeff Atwood's fascination with the sporkfe - a combination spoon, fork, and knife utensil. He notes the Australian commercial version called 'Splayds' has existed for 50 years, but prefers the name 'sporkfe' since people don't typically eat with 'blades.' The post ends with self-deprecating humor about his impulse purchase.
key_points: The sporkfe combines spoon, fork, and knife into one utensil
The concept has been commercialized in Australia as 'Splayds' for 50 years
Atwood prefers 'sporkfe' over 'splade' as a naming choice
The purchase was an impulse buy he can't fully explain
money_quotes: It's a spoon. It's a fork. It's a knife.
Really, when was the last time you ate your food with a blade?
I have no idea why I bought these.
But at least I saved myself from the indignity of owning The Mother of All Swiss Army Knives.
key_insight: Sometimes we're drawn to objects that cleverly combine multiple functions, even when we can't articulate why.
